purpose: Opening Digest must reproduce the test-project-1 styled sample look for captions, logo panel, top-right title, colors, and frame treatment.

frameTreatment.description: Use the same sample-11 frame treatment used by test-project-1 styled preview.

logoPanel.renderRule: Use the project logo file, not OCR text. Preserve the white slanted panel and scale all coordinates from 720p.

contentVideo.note: Use the same 69px downward content shift as the styled sample so faces sit below the header while leaving room for the lower digest captions.

rendering.reason: Supersampling keeps the slanted logo-panel edge and thin bands smooth.

topRightTitle.description: Place the Opening Digest topic/title in the same top-band visual language as the sample/reference title banners.
collisionRules: Do not overlap the left logo panel.
If the title is long, shrink text or split at a natural phrase boundary before reducing header height.

captionStyle.description: Use the same lower catchphrase subtitle style as test-project-1 styled preview.
captionStyle.sourceText: semantic_marks.highlight_candidates[].digest_caption or semantic_marks.punchline_subtitles[] selected for Opening Digest

policy.captionSwapRule: Prefer one short emphasized phrase. If the line is slightly longer, replace the on-screen caption once within the same speech segment. Do not chain multiple swaps to approximate full subtitles.

openingDigestRules.clipStructure: Hard-cut strongest statements and reactions. Use the same frame, logo, title, and caption treatment throughout the digest.

topRightTitleText.rule: During the digest, the upper-right title is the overall title for the whole video, not a chapter title.

openingDigestRules.captionFrequency: Fewer captions than the main section. Prefer one strong phrase per digest clip. One caption swap within the same speech segment is allowed when the line is slightly longer.

transitionOut.rule: After the digest ends, insert company-movie.mp4 before the first main-section event. Trim in/out to the editorially appropriate portion.

validation: All digest captions must fit inside their boxes at preview and final scales.
Captions must not cover the active speaker's mouth or key facial expression.
Logo panel, top band, top-right title, and bottom band must remain visible for every digest shot.
No full transcript subtitles in Opening Digest.
At most one caption swap per speech segment in the digest.

mainSectionStyleOverrides.description: The main section uses the same basic caption, logo, and upper-right title placement as the digest, but removes the digest-only top and bottom bands.
mainSectionStyleOverrides.introductionRule: Participant introduction and self-introduction segments are part of the main section. Detect them from the transcript/transcribed subtitles and speaker turns, then create the appropriate main-section events.
sharedWithDigest: caption font, color, rounded box, reveal animation, and fade timing

removedFromMainSection.note: Keep only the upper-right title's purple box background. Do not draw the digest-style full-width top band or bottom band line in main-section clips.

topRightTitleText.rule: During the main section, the upper-right title changes by chapter/topic. Use the chapter title for the current transcript-derived segment.

captionSource.rule: Use editorial caption subtitles selected from the transcript, not full transcript subtitles.

captionLength.rule: Main section caption subtitles follow the same length policy as the digest: short emphasized phrases by default, with one caption swap allowed when a line is slightly longer.

validation: Main-section renders must not include the digest top full-width purple band.
Main-section renders must not include the digest bottom purple line.
Main-section upper-right title boxes must remain visible and use the same blue-purple title style.
Introduction events must be selected from transcript evidence, not assumed to occur at a fixed timestamp.
Main section must use editorial caption subtitles only; never full transcript subtitles.
At most one caption swap per speech segment in the main section.
